verifier_input.doc_snippet:
The ionosphere is a highly active area of the Earth’s upper atmosphere composed with high concentrations of ions and abundant free electrons [1]. It located between 60 and more than 1000 km above the Earth’s surface. This layer yielding from ionization process through interaction of solar radiation with atmospheric gases, and also driven by cosmic rays, and high energy particles from outer space [2]. The ionized nature of ionosphere permits reflecting, refracting, and modifying signal propagation, offer numerous significant advantages for satellite-based communication and navigation systems. Despite these advantages, the ionosphere poses critical problems for contemporary space-based systems.
